Title: UPL3 promotes BZR1 degradation, growth arrest, and seedling survival under starvation stress in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HECT domain-containing ubiquitin E3 ligase; proteasome-associated ubiquitin ligase; N-terminal Armadillo repeats
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starvation-induced BZR1 degradation, growth arrest, and seedling survival under sugar starvation; loss of UPL3 increases growth under short-term sugar limitation but decreases survival under prolonged starvation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UPL3 was identified as a BZR1-interacting protein by SIL-IP-MS and PRM under starvation conditions. 2. upl3 mutants accumulated more BZR1 and showed BR-hyperresponsive growth phenotypes under sugar-limited conditions. 3. UPL3 protein increased upon starvation and TOR inhibition and decreased after glucose treatment. 4. UPL3 is required for starvation- and TOR-inhibition-induced suppression of growth and for survival during long-term starvation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UPL3 likely acts downstream of sugar-TOR signaling and may promote degradation of already ubiquitinated BZR1, analogous to ubiquitin ligase relay mechanisms reported for NPR1 and EIN3. The study notes prior proteomics evidence that upl3 mutants accumulate lower sugar and starch, but this was not the focal metabolite phenotype directly tested here.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed across plant organs according to discussion/supplemental summary
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Quantitative proteomics (15N SIL-IP-MS); targeted proteomics PRM; PRIDE PXD04613
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: upl3 loss-of-function promotes growth under short-term starvation but dramatically reduces survival under prolonged starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants (upl3-4, upl3-26); co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; immunoblotting of BZR1 accumulation; glucose/starvation and AZD8055 treatments; genetic interaction with tor-es; survival assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sugar starvation/darkness; glucose supplementation; TOR inhibition by AZD8055; estradiol-induced TOR RNAi; PPZ treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering crops to optimize growth under adequate carbon supply while improving resilience to fluctuating sugar availability and starvation stress; caution that reducing UPL3 may increase growth but compromise survival during prolonged starvation

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Growth regulation in response to sugar availability via brassinosteroid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; protein abundance controlled by ubiquitin-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher BZR1 accumulation promotes seedling growth under sugar limitation but reduces survival under prolonged starvation; starvation-induced degradation of BZR1 inhibits growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Starvation and TOR inhibition promote BZR1 degradation. 2. upl3 mutants accumulate both phosphorylated and dephosphorylated BZR1, especially under sugar-free conditions. 3. Stabilized BZR1 correlates with longer hypocotyls under sugar limitation but lower survival during prolonged starvation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BR-responsive transcriptional programs that promote growth
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sugar availability; sugar starvation; TOR signaling; UPL3-mediated ubiquitin-dependent degradation; PPZ-sensitive BR biosynthesis/signaling status
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed across plant organs according to discussion/supplemental summary
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Protein interactome analyzed by 15N SIL-IP-MS and PRM in BZR1-YFP lines; PRIDE PXD04613
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated BZR1 supports growth under sugar limitation but compromises survival under extended starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunoblotting with anti-BZR1; BZR1-CFP/YFP transgenic lines; confocal imaging; genetic analysis in upl3 background; glucose and AZD8055 treatments; survival assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with UPL3 in vivo; BZR1-UPL3 complex supported by coIP and BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on BZR1 protein stability rather than identification of specific downstream metabolic targets. Both phosphorylated and dephosphorylated forms were assessed, with upl3 especially increasing active dephosphorylated BZR1.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sugar starvation/darkness; glucose supplementation; TOR inhibition by AZD8055; PPZ treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating BZR1 stability through upstream regulators such as UPL3 could tune growth–survival balance under carbon fluctuation, but excessive stabilization may impair starvation survival

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Target of rapamycin serine/threonine protein kinase
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sugar-responsive control of growth through regulation of BZR1 stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Signaling-mediated repression of UPL3 accumulation under sugar-sufficient conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TOR activity promotes growth and BZR1 accumulation; TOR inhibition increases UPL3 levels and BZR1 degradation, suppressing growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOR inhibition by AZD8055 or inducible RNAi suppresses seedling growth. 2. upl3 mutation largely suppresses growth inhibition caused by TOR inactivation. 3. TOR inhibition increases UPL3 and promotes BZR1 degradation, indicating TOR acts upstream of UPL3.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly promotes BZR1 accumulation by inhibiting UPL3 under sugar-sufficient conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TOR inactivation arrests growth; the study implies carbon-conservation benefits under starvation but no broader yield data provided
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical inhibition with AZD8055; inducible tor-es RNAi; hypocotyl phenotyping; BZR1 immunoblotting; YFP-UPL3/BZR1-YFP fluorescence analyses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TOR is an upstream signaling component in this paper, not the primary focal nutrient-metabolism gene, but it is experimentally central to defining the UPL3-BZR1 mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glucose supplementation; sugar starvation/darkness; AZD8055; estradiol induction of tor-es
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: The UPL3 module may provide a more targetable downstream lever than TOR itself for engineering growth under fluctuating carbon supply
